title: Select the right adaptation method for the goal
description: A decision path from prompting through PEFT to full fine-tuning and RLHF based on data, budget, and capability needs.
body: Use when deciding how to adapt a model to a need. Do not jump to full fine-tuning or RLHF first — cheaper interventions often suffice and forget less. Actions: try prompting/few-shot, then PEFT/LoRA for behavior or domain shifts, reserve full fine-tuning for large stable corpora, and use preference methods (RLHF/DPO) only when alignment to human preference is the actual goal. Validation signals include a recorded comparison showing the chosen method meets the target and a cheaper rejected alternative. Risk boundary: RLHF and full tuning carry the most cost and forgetting risk; if a lighter method meets the goal, escalate to the heavier one only with explicit justification.
